95: **Argentina**, 1st in **Group J** on 9 points, face **Egypt** — level on 5 points with one other team in **Group G**, position not yet separated — at Atlanta Stadium at 9:00 AM PT, with Messi having become the first player to score seven goals in two separate tournaments during the dramatic 3-2 extra-time win over Cape Verde, while Egypt's centre-back Yasser Ibrahim is ruled out and left-back Karim Hafez is a doubt after being withdrawn in the 80th minute of the Pharaohs' 4-2 penalty shootout win over Australia — Egypt's first knockout victory on merit in 92 years. Argentina winger Nico González carries an ankle sprain and Facundo Medina (cramp) is also a concern, while the two nations have met only once before, a 2-0 friendly in 2008 that Messi missed through injury; the winner advances to the quarter-finals on July 11 in Kansas City to face **Switzerland** or **Colombia**.
96: **Switzerland**, 1st in **Group B** on 7 points, face **Colombia**, 1st in **Group K** on 7 points, at BC Place in Vancouver at 1:00 PM PT — with Colombia's Jhon Córdoba ruled out for the rest of the tournament after tearing his hamstring in the 8th minute of the 1-0 win over Ghana, while Switzerland defender Luca Jaquez (muscle) remains sidelined and midfielder Michel Aebischer is training separately in a race to be fit; the Manzambi-Embolo-Vargas attacking trio have combined for nine goal contributions in the tournament, and **Colombia** have conceded just once all tournament — a group-stage equaliser by Uzbekistan — keeping clean sheets in their last three. **Colombia** beat **Switzerland** 2-0 at the 1994 edition in their only prior meeting at this stage; the winner faces **Argentina** or **Egypt** in the quarter-finals on July 11 in Kansas City.
93: **Portugal**, 2nd in **Group K** on 5 points, face **Spain**, 1st in **Group H** on 7 points, at AT&T Stadium in Arlington at 12:00 PM PT — Mikel Oyarzabal enters with four tournament goals and 23 goal involvements in his last 16 starts for La Roja, and Unai Simón's 519-minute shutout streak is a new all-time tournament record, breaking Walter Zenga's 517 minutes from 1990, but Spain are without Nico Williams (adductor), Yeremy Pino (shoulder), and Víctor Muñoz (thigh) in attack. **Portugal** edged Croatia 2-1 — Ronaldo converting from the spot in the 68th minute for the first knockout-stage goal of his six-tournament career (976 career goals total), then Gonçalo Ramos heading in a 94th-minute winner — while Roberto Martínez reports no injury concerns; across 40-plus all-time meetings Spain lead 18-7, though **Portugal** beat **Spain** on penalties in last summer's Nations League final. The winner advances to the quarter-finals on July 11 in Los Angeles to face **USA** or **Belgium**.
94: **USA**, 1st in **Group D** on 6 points, host **Belgium** — level on 5 points with one other team, position not yet separated — at Seattle Stadium at 5:00 PM PT, with striker Folarin Balogun (three tournament goals) available after his red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina was overturned on appeal; Cristian Roldan (muscle) remains a doubt and Mark McKenzie (foot) requires assessment. **Belgium** needed a 125th-minute Youri Tielemans penalty to complete a 3-2 comeback from 2-0 down against Senegal, with Zeno Debast (leg) absent for the entire tournament, and the Red Devils have won six straight meetings against the USA including a 5-2 friendly in March 2026 — the only American win in seven all-time meetings was a 3-0 result at the inaugural 1930 edition; the last knockout meeting ended 2-1 to Belgium in extra time in 2014, when Tim Howard made 16 saves. The winner faces **Portugal** or **Spain** in the quarter-finals on July 11 in Los Angeles.
